Title: Completion of the cytosolic post-chorismate phenylalanine biosynthetic pathway in plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of numerous aromatic primary and secondary metabolites including phenylpropanoids and floral volatiles
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcripts detected in all floral tissues; previously characterized as cytosolic and active in flowers
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petals/corollas; contributes to emitted floral volatile production
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts phenylpyruvate to phenylalanine in the cytosolic phenylpyruvate pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhPPY-AT is the previously identified cytosolic phenylpyruvate aminotransferase in petunia. 2. Its cytosolic localization motivated the search for upstream cytosolic enzymes completing the pathway. 3. Tyrosine serves as its amino donor, enabling 15N-tyrosine flux tracing of the cytosolic phenylpyruvate pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior gene characterization cited in paper; subcellular localization; enzyme activity assays in non-aqueous fractionation; 15N-tyrosine labeling-based flux analysis logic
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to pathway completion, PhPPY-AT itself was not the main reverse-genetic target in this study's Results; included because it is a core pathway component experimentally used to define flux through the cytosolic route.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; Mass spectrometry-based metabolic flux analysis; no accession reported specifically for this gene
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Daily light/dark cycle; shikimate feeding in pathway analysis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of phenylalanine-derived volatiles and phenylpropanoids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased plastidial export capacity elevates phenylalanine levels and phenylalanine-derived volatile emission
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transient overexpression of PhpCAT increased phenylalanine and phenylalanine-derived volatiles in both wild type and PhCM2-RNAi flowers. 2. This supports feedback regulation within plastidial aromatic amino acid metabolism when export is limiting. 3. PhpCAT was used to probe pathway homeostasis rather than define the core cytosolic pathway step.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in flowers; metabolite profiling; volatile analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a mechanistic probe for plastid-cytosol phenylalanine homeostasis; not a primary subject of experimentation on pathway completion.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: LIS promoter for transient overexpression construct (from prior work/pART27-LIS-PhpCAT)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-MS volatile profiling; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for phenylpropanoids, anthocyanins, salicylic acid, lignin and aromatic volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cytosolic post-chorismate phenylpyruvate pathway for phenylalanine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/entry step of the cytosolic branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct precursor pool for downstream phenylpyruvate and phenylalanine formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for cytosolic phenylalanine biosynthesis; supported as decisive entry step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcripts found in all floral tissues and leaves; in corolla increase after flower opening; rhythmic with highest levels at 19:00 and 23:00 h
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petals/corollas; contributes to floral volatile output
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cytosolic phenylalanine biosynthesis and phenylalanine-derived volatile emission
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhCM2 has much higher chorismate affinity and ~49.7-fold higher catalytic efficiency than plastidial PhCM1. 2. Petal-specific RNAi downregulation reduced cytosolic CM activity, petal phenylalanine, prephenate and arogenate pools, and phenylalanine-derived volatile emission. 3. 15N-tyrosine flux modeling showed strong reduction of cytosolic phenylpyruvate-pathway flux, proving PhCM2 directs carbon into cytosolic phenylalanine biosynthesis. 4. PhCM2 suppression also secondarily lowered plastidial arogenate-pathway flux, indicating pathway crosstalk/homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme kinetics; petal-specific RNAi knockdown; subcellular fraction enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling; GC-MS volatile profiling; 15N-tyrosine labeling metabolic flux analysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Clarkia breweri LIS petal-specific promoter for RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi reduced phenylalanine-derived scent emission and altered tyrosine/tryptophan pools; no broader growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cytosolic CM activity assayed at low chorismate reflected PhCM2 enrichment; the pathway branches from chorismate rather than prephenate.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; GC-MS volatile metabolomics; 15N-isotope flux analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Daily light/dark cycle; shikimate feeding; petal developmental stage post-anthesis

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cytosolic phenylpyruvate pathway contributing to phenylalanine supply under defense demand
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor for phenylalanine used to form phenylacetaldehyde under wounding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Wounding induced AtCM2 about twofold in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for wound-induced phenylacetaldehyde emission, indicating contribution to phenylalanine biosynthesis under stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two T-DNA knockout lines lacked AtCM2 transcript. 2. Under normal conditions aromatic amino acid pools were unchanged in leaves. 3. Upon mechanical wounding, wild type but not cm2 mutants increased phenylacetaldehyde emission. 4. Since AtAAS induction was unaffected, AtCM2 is required to supply phenylalanine substrate for defense-associated volatile production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants; genomic PCR; RT-PCR; qRT-PCR; wound-induced volatile profiling by GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of wound-induced phenylacetaldehyde emission; no basal growth phenotype reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates that CM2 contribution is conserved beyond petunia and becomes important when phenylpropanoid demand increases.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for aromatic specialized metabolites and floral scent
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cytosolic post-chorismate phenylpyruvate pathway for phenylalanine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts prephenate to phenylpyruvate; also retains arogenate dehydratase activity
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream intermediate step between chorismate mutase and phenylpyruvate aminotransferase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of phenylalanine in cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway-completing step; bottleneck status not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT/PDT catalytic domain with ACT regulatory domain
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Dual: plastidial long isoform (PhADT3L) and cytosolic short isoform (PhADT3S)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in flowers; rhythmic expression with day/night cycle; long and short transcript populations present at nearly similar levels
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petals/corollas; linked to floral volatile emission
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides cytosolic PDT activity required for efficient phenylalanine and phenylalanine-derived volatile production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Non-aqueous fractionation showed PDT activity split nearly 1:1 between cytosol and plastids. 2. 5'-RLM-RACE identified an alternative transcription start site generating PhADT3S, a transcript lacking the plastid transit peptide. 3. GFP fusions confirmed cytosolic localization of PhADT3S, while recombinant PhADT3S retained PDT activity similar to PhADT3L. 4. Immunoblot/proteomics detected both ~42 kD and ~33 kD PhADT3 isoforms in petals. 5. Transient RNAi of PhADT3 lowered phenylalanine and phenylalanine-derived volatiles in wild type but not in PhCM2-RNAi flowers, indicating it acts in the same cytosolic pathway as PhCM2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Non-aqueous fractionation enzyme assays; yeast pha2 complementation; 5'-RLM-RACE; absolute qRT-PCR; GFP subcellular localization; recombinant enzyme kinetics; immunoprecipitation/immunoblot; proteomics; transient RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for transient RNAi and GFP assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi reduced phenylalanine and floral volatile emission; no broader developmental penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Alternative transcription start site, not alternative translation initiation alone, underlies cytosolic isoform production. The cytosolic short isoform completes the plant phenylpyruvate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics MassIVE MSV000083169; LC-MS metabolomics; GC-MS volatile profiling

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cytosolic prephenate dehydratase-active short isoform of ADT3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts prephenate to phenylpyruvate in the cytosol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Immediate precursor-forming step upstream of PPY-AT
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT/PDT catalytic domain; ACT regulatory domain; lacks plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Alternative transcript detected in petals; abundance nearly comparable to long transcript; rhythmic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cytosolic isoform completes the cytosolic post-chorismate phenylalanine biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Produced from an alternative transcription start site upstream of the second ATG. 2. Encodes a 33-kD cytosolic protein. 3. GFP localization and recombinant assays confirmed it is a functional cytosolic PDT. 4. Provides the missing intermediate step between PhCM2 and PhPPY-AT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5'-RLM-RACE; qRT-PCR; GFP localization; recombinant enzyme assay; immunoblot/proteomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally the key newly discovered isoform; experimentally embedded within total PhADT3 analyses.
